Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Petition.
Off Calendar Dec-13-2012 Off Calendar

Demurrer Of Paul D. Miller To Will Contest And Grounds Of Opposition To Probate Of Purported Will; Notice Of Hearing; Memorandum In Support Of Demurrer
Off Calendar Nov-15-2012 Text Ruling

Respondent S.A. Breall, Exe. Notice Of Motion And Motion On Demurrer To Will Contest And Grounds Of Opposition To Pribate Of Purported Will.
Off Calendar Nov-15-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joseph Breall for Sophie Breall (Executor). Peter Meyers for Paul Miller. Alfred Bovbish for Contestants.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the Court's tentative ruling. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. David Waterman lacks standing since any interest he might have is derivative of Suzanne Waterman. Suzanne Waterman's contest is time-barred. Mr. Meyers is to prepare the order. The order is to include that upon inquiry of the Petitioner, the Petitioner represented that he was unable to present any basis for leave to amend that would overcome the grounds for demurrer.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Respondent S.A, Breall, Exe., Demurrer To Will Contest And Grounds Of Opposition To Pribate Of Purported Will And Memroamdum Of Points And Authorities.
Off Calendar Nov-15-2012 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion
Off Calendar Aug-02-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alfred Dovbish for Suzanne Waterman as Guardian Ad Litem for David Howard Waterman. David King for Louise Brucia (Petitioner). Matthew Mraule for Paul Miller. Joseph Breall and Jill Diamond for Sophie Breall (Executor).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their respective positions. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling, the Motion of Suzanne Waterman, as Guardian ad Litem for David Howard Waterman, for Enforcement of Right to Discovery and for Sanctions is denied. Appointment as guardian ad litem merely gave Ms. Waterman the power to prosecute or defend actions on behalf of her minor son. It did not make either David Waterman or Suzanne Waterman a party to any action, and neither is a party to the action in question. Ms. Waterman is, however, entitled to purchase a copy of depositions from the court reporter pursuant to CCP section 2025.570. Sanctions are awarded against Ms. Waterman's counsel in favor of Sophie Breall in the amount of $900 and in favor of Louise Brucia Montalbano in the amount of $970. The late-filed opposition filed on behalf of Paul D. Miller will not be considered. Order signed in open court.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Ella Yip.

Petition To Recover Trust Property; To Determine Enforceability Of An Oral Contract To Make A Will; Petition For Contructive Trust
Off Calendar May-02-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joseph Breall for Sophie Breall. Scott Atkinson for Louise Brucia (Petitioner). Peter Myers for Paul Miller also known as Doe 1.

The Petition to Recover Trust Property, etc. was referred to the department of the Presiding Judge (206) to be set for trial, (order for the same signed in open court). Status on trial setting and discovery was taken off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Trial Setting And Discovery
Off Calendar Aug-10-2011 Continued To Jan-11-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mathew Mraule for Paul Miller. David King for Luise Brucia Montalbano. Alfred Dovbish. Joseph Breale, Counsel for the Estate, was also present.

Mr. King stated that they were at the beginning stages of discovery, that there were depositions pending, and that after this round of depositions that there would be at least a half dozen more depositions. Mr. Mraule concurred with Mr. King, stated that they are in the "infancy" regarding discovery, that he was just brought into this case, and that he was requesting a six month continuance. The matter was continued to January 11, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Petition To Recover Trust Property; To Determine Enforceability Of An Oral Contract To Make A Will; Petition For Contructive Trust
Off Calendar Mar-09-2011 Continued To Aug-10-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Respondent For Order Moving Probate Code 850 Action To Civil Division
Off Calendar Mar-02-2011 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joseph Breall for Sophie Breall (Executor). David King for Louise Brucia (Petitioner).

The Court stated that Counsel Breall is related to a fellow bench officer, Judge Susan Breall, and that it can be impartial. No objection by Mr. King. The Court stated that unless the parties were ready for trial that it would not send the matter out and noted that Probate hears its own law and motion matters. Discussions regarding the posture of the case, law and motion, and the possiblity of a single assignment were held. Mr. King stated that there will be a lot of law and motion and that they want to reserve their right to a jury trial. The Court stated that if the matter were sent out that it would go to the Presiding Judge's Department - Department 206 for assignment. Mr. Breall stated that he would need to name a "Doe" Trustee and that the matter should be continued to August. At the conclusion of discussions/argument, the Court denied the motion for order moving probate code 850 to civil division without prejudice (prevailing party to prepare the order), and continued the petition to recover trust property to August. Mr. King stated that they are serving discovery and that there will probably be a lot of depositions. The Court set August 10, 2011 for status on trial setting and status on discovery.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: none.
